title: Update working memory from GitHub Copilot Responses API session
summary: Durable provider context learned while adding GitHub Copilot Responses API support and addressing review feedback.

claims.text: openai_compat now owns shared Responses API helpers: system messages are extracted into top-level instructions, non-system messages are converted into Responses input, Responses SSE events are converted into StreamEvent deltas/tool events/errors/done, and non-streaming Responses output items parse into CompletionResponse.

The GitHub Copilot provider supports the Responses API for models matching gpt-5.4*, gpt-5.2-pro, and codex-*; its non-streaming path posts to /responses with Responses input, optional instructions, and flat Responses tool definitions, while its streaming path posts stream=true to /responses and parses SSE through the shared Responses stream state.

For Copilot models not preclassified as Responses-only, both non-streaming and streaming chat-completions paths fall back to /responses when a 400 response body case-insensitively matches unsupported_api_for_model or says the model is not accessible via /chat/completions.

OpenAiProvider no longer has private Responses instruction/input splitting or output-index resolution helpers; its Responses path imports split_responses_instructions_and_input and responses_output_index from openai_compat while keeping its existing WebSocket stream handling local.

The session chose a Copilot-specific HTTP+SSE Responses path instead of reusing the OpenAI provider's WebSocket Responses implementation, because the OpenAI path was gated to api.openai.com and Copilot only needed the simpler HTTP+SSE flow.
Copilot Responses fallback error matching should remain case-insensitive; this was the actionable review feedback fixed after PR review.

The PR review suggestion to add ProviderRaw events to Responses SSE parsing was intentionally not implemented; process_responses_sse_line remains aligned with the existing OpenAI Responses WebSocket path rather than adding raw-provider events only for Copilot.

metadata.reason: The session plan explicitly stated that the existing OpenAI Responses support was WebSocket-based and api.openai.com-gated, and directed Copilot to use its own simpler HTTP+SSE Responses path.
After PR review, the session identified case-insensitive Copilot Responses fallback matching as the actionable bot comment and fixed it.
The session explicitly skipped the review suggestion to add ProviderRaw events because the existing OpenAI Responses WebSocket path had the same omission.
